title: Damped Harmonic Motion Formula
text: The general form for damped harmonic motion is f(t) $$=$$ ae**(-ct)\cos(\omegat) or f(t) $$=$$ ae**(-ct)\sin(\omegat). For problems where the initial displacement is given as a maximum, we often use f(t) $$=$$ $${ae}^{\left(-ct\right)}$$ to model the envelope of the damping.

title: Identify Initial Amplitude
text: What is the initial displacement of the string from its resting position?

title: Set up the Equation
text: Substitute the known values (initial amplitude, time, and displacement at that time) into the envelope equation f(t) $$=$$ $${ae}^{\left(-ct\right)}$$.

title: Calculate Damping Constant
text: What is the value of c?
